title: Ambientoperator
summary: Packages the abstract ambient operator calculus: Q, the Euler operator X, the ambient Laplacian, powers, homogeneity, and commutator laws.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages the abstract ambient operator calculus: Q, the Euler operator X, the ambient Laplacian, powers, homogeneity, and commutator laws.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/Ambientoperator.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Real-valued functions on the ambient bundle. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Conf.Ambient Bundle maps to the real numbers.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev Function : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise zero function. In explicit terms, its defining expression is fun maps to 0.
correspondence.leanType: def zero : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise addition. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to f U plus g U.
correspondence.leanType: def add (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise subtraction. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to f U minus g U.
correspondence.leanType: def sub (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise multiplication. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to f U times g U.
correspondence.leanType: def mul (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Scalar multiplication of an ambient function. In explicit terms, for c in the real numbers, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to c times f U.
correspondence.leanType: def smul (c : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise natural power. In explicit terms, for f of type function conf, and m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is the function sending U to f U to the power m.
correspondence.leanType: def pow (f : Function Conf) (m : ℕ) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal ambient calculus data attached to the existing ambient bundle. The field straight records that this calculus is being used in straight ambient form. The fields Q, X, and laplacian are the three quantities entering the ambient algebraic identities. Its mathematical data are: straight: Conf.Straight Form; base Dim: the real numbers; Q: Function Conf; X: Function Conf maps to Function Conf; laplacian: Function Conf maps to Function Conf.
correspondence.leanType: structure Calculus

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal dimension parameter. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.base Dim.
correspondence.leanType: def n : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplication by Q. In explicit terms, for f of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U times f U.
correspondence.leanType: def Qmul (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplication by Q^m. In explicit terms, for m in the natural numbers, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U to the power m times f U.
correspondence.leanType: def QpowMul (m : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A function vanishes modulo Q if it is divisible by Q. In explicit terms, for f of type function conf, its defining expression is there exists h : Function Conf, f equals Cal Conf.Qmul h.
correspondence.leanType: def VanishesModQ (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Equality modulo Q. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Vanishes Mod Q the function sending U to f U minus g U.
correspondence.leanType: def EqModQ (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.Eq Mod Q f f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem EqModQ_refl (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.EqModQ f f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The ambient Laplacian. In explicit terms, for f of type function conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.ambient Laplacian f.
correspondence.leanType: def lap (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Iterated ambient Laplacian. This definition deliberately uses Nat.rec instead of recursive notation through namespace projections. It is more stable in Lean. CalConf.lapPow r f means Δ̃^r f. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is Nat.rec f fun g maps to Cal Conf.lap g r.
correspondence.leanType: def lapPow (r : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian 0 f equals f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_zero (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow 0 f = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, and f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian r plus 1 f equals Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian r f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_succ (r : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow (r + 1) f = CalConf.lap (CalConf.lapPow r f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Infinitesimal homogeneity of weight w. This is the formal version of X f = w f. In explicit terms, for w in the real numbers, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.X f equals the function sending U to w times f U.
correspondence.leanType: def IsXHomogeneous (w : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The already existing ambient density can be checked against infinitesimal homogeneity using X. Important: this is only for real-valued ambient densities, because X acts on real-valued ambient functions. In explicit terms, for χ of type weight the real numbers, w in the real numbers, and d of type ambient.equivariant.density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w d.to Fun.
correspondence.leanType: def IsXHomogeneousDensity {χ : Weight ℝ} (w : ℝ) (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The underlying real-valued ambient function of an existing ambient density. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, χ of type weight the real numbers, and d of type ambient.equivariant.density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is d.to Fun.
correspondence.leanType: def densityToFunction (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) {χ : Weight ℝ} (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For χ of type Weight the real numbers, d of type Ambient.Equivariant.Density Conf : equals Conf χ, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, Cal Conf.density To Function d U equals d U.
correspondence.leanType: theorem densityToFunction_apply {χ : Weight ℝ} (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.densityToFunction d U = d U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For χ of type Weight the real numbers, w in the real numbers, and d of type Ambient.Equivariant.Density Conf : equals Conf χ, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous Density w d if and only if Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w d.to Fun.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsXHomogeneousDensity_iff {χ : Weight ℝ} (w : ℝ) (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneousDensity w d ↔ CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w d.toFun

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal algebraic identities among Q, X, and the ambient Laplacian. These are recorded as hypotheses at the current abstraction level. Later, after a genuine ambient metric, connection, and Laplacian are available, these fields can be replaced by actual theorems. Its mathematical data are: X Q: Cal Conf.X Cal Conf.Q equals the function sending U to 2 times Cal Conf.Q U; lap smul: for every c f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap the function sending U to c times f U equals the function sending U to c times Cal Conf.lap f U; lap add: for every f g : Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap the function sending U to f U plus g U equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.lap f U plus Cal Conf.lap g U; X lap comm: for every f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.X Cal Conf.lap f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.X f U minus 2 times Cal Conf.lap f U; lap Q comm: for every f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.Qmul f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U times Cal Conf.lap f U plus 2 times 2 times Cal Conf.X f U plus the dimension n of the ambient calculus plus 2 times f U; lap Qpow of XHomogeneous: for every m w : the real numbers f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w f maps to Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.Qpow Mul m f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U to the power m times Cal Conf.lap f U plus 2 times m times 2 times w plus the dimension n of the ambient calculus plus 2 times m times Cal Conf.Q U to the power m minus 1 times f U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{X\_Q}&\colon X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} = U \longmapsto 2 \cdot Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U\\\operatorname{lap\_smul}&\colon \forall c (f \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}), \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (U \longmapsto c \cdot f U) = U \longmapsto c \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U\\\operatorname{lap\_add}&\colon \forall f g \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (U \longmapsto f U + g U) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} g U\\\operatorname{X\_lap\_comm}&\colon \forall f \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}, X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} f) U - 2 \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U\\\operatorname{lap\_Q\_comm}&\colon \forall f \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{Qmul} f) = U \longmapsto Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + 2 \cdot (2 \cdot X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} f U + (n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} + 2) \cdot f U)\\\operatorname{lap\_Qpow\_of\_XHomogeneous}&\colon \forall m {w \colon \mathbb{R}} {f \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}}, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} w f \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{QpowMul} m f) = U \longmapsto Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U ^ m \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + 2 \cdot m \cdot (2 \cdot w + n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} + 2 \cdot m ) \cdot Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U ^ (m - 1) \cdot f U\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure AlgebraicIdentities : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, c in the real numbers, and f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap the function sending U to c times f U equals the function sending U to c times Cal Conf.lap f U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;c\colon \mathbb{R},\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (U \longmapsto c \cdot f U) = U \longmapsto c \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_smul (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (c : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => c * f U) = fun U => c * CalConf.lap f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap the function sending U to f U plus g U equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.lap f U plus Cal Conf.lap g U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (U \longmapsto f U + g U) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} g U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_add (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => f U + g U) = fun U => CalConf.lap f U + CalConf.lap g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, Cal Conf.X Cal Conf.Q equals the function sending U to 2 times Cal Conf.Q U.
correspondence.leanType: theorem X_Q (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) : CalConf.X CalConf.Q = fun U => 2 * CalConf.Q U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.X Cal Conf.lap f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.X f U minus 2 times Cal Conf.lap f U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} f) U - 2 \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U
correspondence.leanType: theorem X_lap_comm (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.X (CalConf.lap f) = fun U => CalConf.lap (CalConf.X f) U - 2 * CalConf.lap f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.Qmul f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U times Cal Conf.lap f U plus 2 times 2 times Cal Conf.X f U plus the dimension n of the ambient calculus plus 2 times f U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{Qmul} f) = U \longmapsto Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + 2 \cdot (2 \cdot X_{\mathrm{CalConf}} f U + (n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} + 2) \cdot f U)
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_Q_comm (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (CalConf.Qmul f) = fun U => CalConf.Q U * CalConf.lap f U + 2 * (2 * CalConf.X f U + (CalConf.n + 2) * f U)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, m in the natural numbers, w in the real numbers, f of type Function Conf, and hf of type Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w f, Cal Conf.lap Cal Conf.Qpow Mul m f equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.Q U to the power m times Cal Conf.lap f U plus 2 times m times 2 times w plus the dimension n of the ambient calculus plus 2 times m times Cal Conf.Q U to the power m minus 1 times f U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;m\colon \mathbb{N},\;w\colon \mathbb{R},\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hf\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} w f,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{QpowMul} m f) = U \longmapsto Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U ^ m \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U + 2 \cdot m \cdot (2 \cdot w + n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} + 2 \cdot m ) \cdot Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U ^ (m - 1) \cdot f U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_Qpow_of_XHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (m : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.lap (CalConf.QpowMul m f) = fun U => CalConf.Q U ^ m * CalConf.lap f U + 2 * (m : ℝ) * (2 * w + CalConf.n + 2 * (m : ℝ)) * CalConf.Q U ^ (m - 1) * f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.lap the function sending U to f U minus g U equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.lap f U minus Cal Conf.lap g U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} (U \longmapsto f U - g U) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} f U - \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lap} g U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_sub (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => f U - g U) = fun U => CalConf.lap f U - CalConf.lap g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k the function sending U to f U minus g U equals the function sending U to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k f U minus Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k g U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k (U \longmapsto f U - g U) = U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k f U - \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k g U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_sub (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow k (fun U => f U - g U) = fun U => CalConf.lapPow k f U - CalConf.lapPow k g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If f is homogeneous of weight w, then Δ̃ f is homogeneous of weight w - 2. This follows from the commutator relation between X and Δ̃. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_isXHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (w - 2) (CalConf.lap f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Predicate saying that Δ̃^r f has weight w - 2r. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, w in the real numbers, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w minus 2 times r Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian r f.
correspondence.leanType: def LapPowHasWeight (r : ℕ) (w : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weight bookkeeping for iterated ambient Laplacians. If X f = w f, then X (Δ̃^r f) = (w - 2r) Δ̃^r f. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_isXHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (r : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.LapPowHasWeight r w f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Iterated commutator formula for powers of the ambient Laplacian applied to a single Q-multiple. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;w\colon \mathbb{R},\;h\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hh\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} w h,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{Qmul} h) = U \longmapsto Q_{\mathrm{CalConf}} U \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k h U + 2 \cdot k \cdot (2 \cdot w + n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} + 2 - 2 \cdot ((k - 1 \colon \mathbb{R}))) \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} (k - 1) h U
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_Qmul_formula (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {h : Function Conf} (hh : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w h) : CalConf.lapPow k (CalConf.Qmul h) = fun U => CalConf.Q U * CalConf.lapPow k h U + 2 * (k : ℝ) * (2 * w + CalConf.n + 2 - 2 * (((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ))) * CalConf.lapPow (k - 1) h U

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
